text: The glass and steel of the architecture around me feels modern and subtly sterile. I am surrounded by reflections and silence—a liminal, transitional space between outside concerns and internal curiosity. I cross into the vivid R+D area, met by bold colors and interactive displays. My mind sharpens, intrigued by the intersection of technology, human experience, and experimental knowledge. I feel like a wanderer who has slipped through the membrane of ordinary life into a zone where ideas are actively assembled, dissected, and remade. My presence here asks: How will I respond to these provocations about the future? I am both participant and observer, questioning the implications of innovation on what it means to be human.
